features.title: Anonymous access and REST create status parity
features.description: Recover allow-anon behavior and REST create-status projection so runtime and docs derive from one canonical outcome, per adr/ADR-0031 and specs/status-auth-and-error-parity.md.

features.title: AppSpec and harness Uvicorn E2E parity
features.description: Recover parity between AppSpec materialization, imperative harness assembly, and Uvicorn-backed REST/JSON-RPC execution per adr/ADR-0030 and specs/appspec-harness-uvicorn-parity.md.

features.title: Binding-driven REST and JSON-RPC ingress materialization
features.description: Track binding-driven concrete ingress materialization for REST and JSON-RPC without transport-owned semantic dispatch.

features.title: Canonical documentation support surfaces

features.title: Engine resolver and multi-table RPC parity under Uvicorn
features.description: Recover engine resolver precedence, mounted-prefix behavior, and multi-table REST/JSON-RPC parity under Uvicorn, per adr/ADR-0030 and specs/appspec-harness-uvicorn-parity.md.

features.title: Error parity, validation parity, and response structure
features.description: Recover REST/JSON-RPC error parity, validation parity, and response-envelope structure under the canonical projection rules defined in adr/ADR-0031 and specs/status-auth-and-error-parity.md.

features.title: Canonical HTTP route registration
features.description: Tracks the binding-first REST decorator surface and imperative route normalization owned by tigrbl_concrete, including explicit HttpRestBindingSpec defaults, ordinary documentation routes, and the neutral __tigrbl_route_ops__ spec-graph carrier.

features.title: Endpoint-keyed JSON-RPC multiplexing
features.description: Track endpoint-keyed JSON-RPC binding identity, concrete endpoint mounting, and default endpoint ownership in tigrbl_core.

features.title: Kernel bootstrap and plan idempotence parity
features.description: Recover kernel bootstrap, priming, and compiled-plan idempotence invariants called out in adr/ADR-0030 and specs/appspec-harness-uvicorn-parity.md.

features.title: KernelPlan and atoms own transport lookup and matching
features.description: Track the return of transport lookup, endpoint resolution, and operation matching to the compiled KernelPlan and atom chain.

features.title: Canonical datatype catalog and semantic center
features.description: DataTypeSpec and StorageTypeRef define the canonical datatype catalog and semantic center on the active next-target line. Package owner `pkgs/core/tigrbl_core` and crate owner `crates/tigrbl_rs_spec` define the tracked implementation surface.

features.title: ColumnSpec datatype attachment point
features.description: ColumnSpec datatype is the canonical datatype attachment point on the active next-target line. Package owner `pkgs/core/tigrbl_core` and crate owner `crates/tigrbl_rs_spec` define the tracked implementation surface.

features.title: Datatype adapter and registry contract
features.description: TypeAdapter, BaseTypeAdapter, and TypeRegistry provide the executable datatype adapter and registry contract on the active next-target line. Package owner `pkgs/core/tigrbl_core` and crate owner `crates/tigrbl_rs_spec` define the tracked implementation surface.

features.title: Engine datatype lowering and registry bridge
features.description: EngineTypeLowerer, EngineRegistry, and EngineDatatypeBridge control engine-owned datatype lowering and registry bridging on the active next-target line. Package owner `pkgs/core/tigrbl_core` and crate owner `crates/tigrbl_rs_spec` define the tracked implementation surface.

features.title: Reflected datatype mapper and reverse mapping
features.description: ReflectedTypeMapper governs reflected datatype reverse mapping and reflection import behavior on the active next-target line. Package owner `pkgs/core/tigrbl_core` and crate owner `crates/tigrbl_rs_spec` define the tracked implementation surface.

features.title: Default canonical engine-family datatype alignment
features.description: Default relational, dataframe, file, cache, and probabilistic engine families align to the datatype bridge contract on the active next-target line. Package owner `pkgs/engines` and crate owner `crates/tigrbl_rs_engine_inmemory` define the tracked implementation surface.

features.title: Multi-engine table portability and interoperability
features.description: Multi-engine table portability and interoperability rules are specified as governed next-target work on the active line. Package owner `pkgs/core/tigrbl_core` and crate owner `crates/tigrbl_rs_spec` define the tracked implementation surface.

features.title: Schema reflection round-trip recovery
features.description: Schema reflection round-trip recovery is defined with best-effort and metadata-preserving modes on the active next-target line. Package owner `pkgs/core/tigrbl_core` and crate owner `crates/tigrbl_rs_spec` define the tracked implementation surface.

features.title: Remove non-conforming transport bypasses
features.description: Track removal of app-local HTTP dispatch and other transport bypasses that avoid the kernel-owned dispatch path.

features.title: Transport-dispatch governance track setup
features.description: Create the governed transport-dispatch track resources, boundary, validator, and setup evidence before implementation delivery starts.

features.title: REST and JSON-RPC parity through the shared dispatch path
features.description: Track restoration of REST and JSON-RPC semantic parity through the shared transport-dispatch path without test-only relaxation.

features.title: CLI target resolution and app loading
features.description: Track CLI target grammar and loader parity for <module:attr> and <path.py:attr>, including Windows absolute-path handling.

features.title: Kernel priming and OpView cache lifecycle
features.description: Track default-kernel identity, idempotent priming, and on-demand OpView cache semantics across docs, harness, and transport ingress.

features.title: Declared-surface docs extension schema
features.description: Track governed x-tigrbl-surface extension fields so OpenAPI and OpenRPC emit the same declared binding metadata.

features.title: Operator-surface framing and multipart semantics
features.description: Track stable streaming, SSE, multipart, and upload semantics across the governed operator surface.

features.title: Diagnostics endpoint contracts
features.description: Track the stable public contract for framework-owned diagnostics endpoints, including payload shape, warning policy, and system-prefix mounting.

features.title: Canonical analytical verb family
features.description: Track the governed analytical verb family for aggregate and group_by and its runtime/docs parity obligations.

features.title: Canonical realtime and transfer verb family
features.description: Track the governed realtime, transfer, and checkpoint verb family and its runtime/docs parity obligations.

features.title: CLI module target resolution
features.description: Resolve <module:attr> targets without path-style parsing side effects.

features.title: CLI path target resolution
features.description: Resolve <path.py:attr> targets including Windows absolute paths without corrupting the drive-letter segment.

features.title: CLI target surface loading
features.description: Load app objects, router objects, and zero-arg factories from resolved CLI targets.

features.title: CLI target error classification
features.description: Classify loader failures as target-resolution errors rather than unrelated module corruption.

features.title: CLI shared target loader
features.description: Use one loader implementation across command execution and server-selection entrypoints.

features.title: Anonymous access projection
features.description: Project allow-anon behavior from canonical security metadata into runtime and generated docs consistently.

features.title: REST create success status projection
features.description: Project canonical create success to REST 201 Created unless an explicit operation contract overrides it.

features.title: Authorization failure projection parity
features.description: Project authentication and authorization failures consistently across REST and JSON-RPC for equivalent canonical outcomes.

features.title: Validation failure projection parity
features.description: Project canonical validation failures consistently across REST and JSON-RPC transports.

features.title: Error envelope structure parity
features.description: Preserve response-envelope structure and error-body shape consistently across success and failure paths.

features.title: AppSpec and imperative plan parity
features.description: Compile equivalent AppSpec and imperative declarations into the same semantic kernel plan.

features.title: Harness and runtime routing parity
features.description: Use the same compiled routing and projection rules in harness execution and ordinary runtime execution.

features.title: Uvicorn REST and JSON-RPC semantic parity
features.description: Preserve the same canonical semantic outcome for REST and JSON-RPC under Uvicorn-backed execution.

features.title: JSON-RPC notification 204 projection
features.description: Project JSON-RPC notifications to 204 No Content while request/response calls retain JSON-RPC envelopes.

features.title: Engine resolver precedence
features.description: Apply engine resolver precedence deterministically before final transport projection.

features.title: Mounted prefix routing parity
features.description: Preserve equivalent routing behavior for mounted and prefixed Uvicorn scenarios.

features.title: Multi-table routing parity
features.description: Preserve multi-table routing and projection behavior consistently across REST and JSON-RPC.

features.title: Default kernel identity
features.description: Maintain one logical default-kernel identity for a materialized app or router plan scope.

features.title: ensure_primed idempotence
features.description: Allow repeated ensure_primed calls without losing or duplicating the usable default kernel.

features.title: OpView on-demand cache reuse
features.description: Reuse the canonical OpView cache and kernel identity when get_opview materializes on demand.

features.title: Kernel cache invalidation contract
features.description: Require explicit invalidation before cache replacement or default-kernel reset.

features.title: Declared-surface binding family emission
features.description: Emit x-tigrbl-surface.binding.family whenever a surface is bound to a declared transport family.

features.title: Declared-surface binding proto emission
features.description: Emit x-tigrbl-surface.binding.proto whenever a concrete protocol binding is declared.

features.title: Declared-surface optional metadata emission
features.description: Emit exchange, framing, subevents, and txScope only when those declarations exist on the bound surface.

features.title: Declared-surface cross-projection parity
features.description: Keep OpenAPI and OpenRPC declared-surface metadata aligned for shared transport declarations.

features.title: Stream chunk order preservation
features.description: Preserve stream chunk ordering and multi-chunk body emission rather than collapsing chunks into one accidental body.

features.title: SSE event framing
features.description: Emit WHATWG event-stream bodies with framing compatible with produced event boundaries.

features.title: Multipart form preservation
features.description: Preserve form fields and multipart values without transport-specific loss during parsing.

features.title: Uploaded file runtime contract
features.description: Preserve uploaded filename, content-type, and body access for runtime handlers.

features.title: Diagnostics system-prefix mount
features.description: Mount framework-owned diagnostics endpoints beneath the configured system prefix.

features.title: Healthz stable payload
features.description: Return a stable health payload from /system/healthz rather than an environment-specific debug dump.

features.title: Diagnostics warning vocabulary
features.description: Restrict diagnostics warnings to a governed vocabulary and suppress undocumented absence warnings.

features.title: Kernelz bounded payload
features.description: Expose kernel and priming status through a bounded operational payload rather than raw object state.

features.title: Analytical family membership freeze
features.description: Freeze the canonical analytical family to the declared aggregate and group_by verbs only.

features.title: Analytical runtime/docs/export parity
features.description: Keep runtime dispatch, docs builders, and default package exports aligned with the canonical analytical verb family.

features.title: Realtime and transfer family membership freeze
features.description: Freeze the canonical realtime and transfer family to the declared publish, subscribe, tail, upload, download, append_chunk, send_datagram, and checkpoint verbs only.

features.title: Realtime and transfer runtime/docs/export parity
features.description: Keep runtime dispatch, docs builders, and default package exports aligned with the canonical realtime and transfer verb family.

features.title: Kernel priming runtime parity
features.description: Apply the same priming and cache semantics in sync harness execution and async runtime execution.

features.title: Declared-surface metadata omission contract
features.description: Omit undeclared x-tigrbl-surface metadata fields instead of synthesizing transport-specific guesses.

features.title: Operator-surface smoke/runtime parity
features.description: Preserve the same governed operator-surface semantics in docs-backed smoke paths and live runtime execution.

features.title: Diagnostics absence-warning suppression
features.description: Suppress warnings for absent optional integrations unless the warning is explicitly governed.

features.title: Engine extension surface
features.description: Tracks the governed engine extension surface as a separate SSOT family spanning engine contracts, plugin resolution, datatype lowering, and public/base projections.

features.title: Engine surface contract and datatype bridge
features.description: Tracks the engine-owned contract surface spanning EngineSpec, EngineProviderSpec, and the datatype bridge/lowering contracts.

features.title: Engine plugin registration and resolution
features.description: Tracks engine plugin discovery, registration, and precedence-driven resolution across app, router, table, and op scopes.

features.title: Engine public and base projection
features.description: Tracks public and base projection of engine-facing contracts through tigrbl and tigrbl_base.

features.title: Hook extension surface
features.description: Tracks the governed hook extension surface as a separate SSOT family spanning phase legality, selector semantics, attachment/order behavior, and public/base projections.

features.title: Hook phase and selector contract
features.description: Tracks the hook phase vocabulary and selector semantics as part of the governed hook surface.

features.title: Hook attachment and ordering behavior
features.description: Tracks hook attachment through app/router/table/op declarations together with deterministic hook execution ordering.

features.title: Hook public and base projection
features.description: Tracks public and base projection of hook-facing contracts and helpers.

features.title: Op extension surface
features.description: Tracks the governed op extension surface as a separate SSOT family spanning canonical verb families, custom ops, and public projection of op-facing contracts.

features.title: Default canonical OLTP op family
features.description: Tracks the frozen default canonical OLTP op family as part of the governed op extension surface.

features.title: Custom op extension contract
features.description: Tracks the governed custom-op extension point distinct from the canonical verb families.

features.title: Op public projection
features.description: Tracks public projection of op-facing contracts and helpers through the tigrbl facade.

features.title: Middleware extension surface
features.description: Tracks the governed middleware extension surface as a separate SSOT family spanning protocol/composition rules, bounded catalog policy, public projection, and auth-surface separation.

features.title: Middleware protocol and composition
features.description: Tracks middleware protocol compatibility and deterministic wrapper composition as part of the middleware extension surface.

features.title: Middleware built-in catalog boundary
features.description: Tracks the bounded built-in middleware catalog consisting of Middleware and CORSMiddleware.

features.title: Middleware public projection
features.description: Tracks public projection of middleware-facing contracts and decorators through the tigrbl facade.

features.title: Middleware and auth-surface separation
features.description: Tracks the governed non-goal that generic auth remains dependency/hook-based rather than becoming a monolithic middleware abstraction.

features.description: Tracks AliasSpec as a first-class tigrbl_core._spec contract surface.

features.title: Mapping dispatch convergence
features.description: Keep canonical mapping and route/RPC projection code from importing removed dispatch modules or invoking semantic operations through transport bypasses.

features.title: op_ctx table owner-scope materialization
features.description: Track table-owned op_ctx declarations materializing through include_table into Uvicorn-backed REST execution across arity, response schema, and persist-policy variants.

features.title: op_ctx router owner-scope materialization
features.description: Track router-scoped inclusion of table op_ctx declarations into Uvicorn-backed REST execution across arity, response schema, and persist-policy variants.

features.title: op_ctx app owner-scope materialization
features.description: Track app-local op_ctx declarations materializing into Uvicorn-backed REST execution without table ownership across arity, response schema, and persist-policy variants.

features.title: Pre-TX dependency execution
features.description: Track ordinary operation dependencies executing in PRE_TX_BEGIN before transaction start, handler execution, persistence, or commit side effects.

features.title: Pre-TX security dependency execution
features.description: Track operation security dependencies executing before ordinary dependencies and before transaction start, including auth failure behavior.

features.title: Operation diagnostics projection
features.description: Track diagnostics projecting materialized operation, hook, atom, dependency, persistence, and kernel-step state without redefining execution semantics.

features.title: RFC 8785 JCS conformance vectors
features.description: Add golden vectors for ordering, string escaping, unicode, and numeric canonicalization to assert byte-stable JCS output.

features.title: RFC 8785 JCS canonicalizer
features.description: Provide a governed canonicalization path that emits RFC 8785 JCS canonical UTF-8 JSON bytes for proof-bound payloads.

features.title: RFC 8785 JCS rejection semantics
features.description: Define deterministic fail-closed behavior for non-JCS inputs such as non-finite numbers and unsupported JSON forms.

features.title: JSON-RPC batch framing
features.description: Tracks JSON-RPC batch request ordering, per-item result/error isolation, and batch envelope behavior as integration-governed runtime semantics.

features.title: Deprecated export compatibility window
features.description: Tracks the policy that deprecated public exports remain available only inside declared compatibility windows and are removed after the target boundary.

features.title: Uvicorn protocol-mode runtime parity
features.description: Tracks that Uvicorn protocol modes preserve Tigrbl-owned routing, dispatch, status, validation, and error semantics.

features.title: Documentation mount runtime surface parity
features.description: Tracks OpenAPI, OpenRPC, Swagger, Lens, and favicon documentation mounts across app/router Uvicorn forms and their runtime/docs parity obligations.
Tracks registration and feature linkage for all integration tests under pkgs/core/tigrbl_tests/tests/i9n.

tests.title: HTTP route websocket binding metadata slice

claims.title: REST and JSON-RPC ingress materialization are binding-driven on the transport-dispatch track.
claims.description: The governed transport-dispatch track defines REST and JSON-RPC ingress materialization from op-centric binding declarations rather than transport-owned dispatch logic.

claims.title: The active 0.3.19.dev1 line is not certifiably fully featured.
The active 0.3.19.dev1 line is not certifiably fully RFC compliant.
Engine-family datatype alignment lacks feature-specific proof slices.
Rust backend claims must remain blocked until Python-vs-Rust parity lanes pass as release evidence.
Tigrcorn interop and benchmark certification claims must remain blocked until governed release evidence populates the public bundle targets.
Tigrcorn hardening and negative-certification claims must remain blocked until profile-specific negative corpora run as governed release evidence.
The active line cannot be promoted until claim lifecycle prerequisites and preserved release evidence are complete.
Stable release 0.3.18 remains the frozen current certification boundary.
OpenAPI 3.1.0 and JSON Schema Draft 2020-12 emission remain in boundary for the frozen release.
JSON-RPC 2.0 and OpenRPC 1.2.6 framework surfaces remain in the frozen release boundary.
Retained RFC 7235, RFC 7617, and RFC 6750 rows remain current-boundary claims.
Operator surfaces closed in Phase 7 remain current-boundary claims.
Unified tigrbl CLI command and flag surface remains current-boundary scope.
Active line 0.3.19.dev1 exists as governed next-target work only and is not a certified release.
OpenAPI, OpenRPC, AsyncAPI, and JSON Schema read from canonical metadata sources.
claims.description: OpenAPI consumes route-facing metadata from Route plus bindings/models/schemas, while OpenRPC, AsyncAPI, and JSON Schema continue to derive from their canonical op, binding, and schema inputs without a docs-owned route registry.

claims.title: Documentation endpoints mount as ordinary routes and normalized route-backed ops.
claims.description: Documentation and system endpoints are mounted as ordinary imperative routes, set inherit_owner_dependencies=False where required, and normalize into the same __tigrbl_route_ops__ carrier without any surface_kind abstraction.

claims.title: Gate A through Gate E evidence exists for stable release 0.3.18.
Release-grade claim rows are mapped to evidence artifacts.
Frozen release 0.3.18 remains honestly describable as certifiably fully featured within the declared boundary.
Frozen release 0.3.18 remains honestly describable as certifiably fully RFC/spec compliant within the declared boundary.
Executors are governed to execute compiled plans only and not perform transport lookup or matching.
claims.description: The transport-dispatch track records executor non-ownership for route and RPC matching and limits executors to compiled-plan execution.

claims.title: Factories are canonical and shortcuts re-export factory aliases.
claims.description: The canonical construction surface lives under factories for make*/define*/derive* helpers and direct factory aliases such as acol, vcol, and op, while shortcuts remains a thin re-export layer.

claims.title: HTTP routes are concrete op aliases with HttpRestBindingSpec bindings.
claims.description: Binding-first REST decorators materialize concrete custom ops through op_ctx(bindings=(HttpRestBindingSpec(...), ...)), and imperative HTTP routes normalize equivalent route-backed ops into the owner-local __tigrbl_route_ops__ spec carrier.

claims.title: System document HTTP routes use the same concrete registration path as user routes.
claims.description: OpenAPI, OpenRPC, AsyncAPI, JSON Schema, Swagger, Lens, favicon, and diagnostics endpoints mount as ordinary routes and normalize into the same route-backed op carrier as user-defined imperative routes.

claims.title: Deprecated tigrbl_canon HTTP route registration surfaces are removed from the active line.
claims.description: The active line exposes no separate HTTP route registration subsystem: module-level route_ctx(...) and route(...) are removed, register_http_route(...) is absent, and route normalization happens through ordinary decorators and imperative route ownership.

claims.title: JSON-RPC multiplexed ingress uses endpoint-keyed bindings and core-owned default endpoint mappings.
claims.description: The transport-dispatch track records endpoint-keyed JSON-RPC binding identity and core-owned default endpoint constants and mappings.

claims.title: KernelPlan and atoms are the governed owners of transport lookup and matching for the transport-dispatch track.
claims.description: The track explicitly assigns transport lookup, binding resolution, and op matching to KernelPlan compilation and atom execution rather than runtime or executor dispatch code.

claims.title: DataTypeSpec and StorageTypeRef define the active canonical semantic datatype center for the next target.
ColumnSpec.datatype is the canonical datatype attachment point on the active next-target line.
TypeAdapter, BaseTypeAdapter, and TypeRegistry provide the executable datatype contract on the active next-target line.
EngineTypeLowerer, EngineRegistry, and EngineDatatypeBridge control engine-owned lowering on the active next-target line.
ReflectedTypeMapper governs reverse mapping and reflection import behavior on the active next-target line.
Relational, dataframe, file, cache, and probabilistic engine families align to the datatype bridge contract on the active next-target line.
Table portability and interoperability rules are specified as governed next-target work on the active line.
Reflection-driven round-trip schema recovery is defined with best-effort and metadata-preserving modes on the active next-target line.
The canonical `create` op is governed as a distinct executable claim on the active next-target line.
The canonical `read` op is governed as a distinct executable claim on the active next-target line.
The canonical `update` op is governed as a distinct executable claim on the active next-target line.
The canonical `replace` op is governed as a distinct executable claim on the active next-target line.

The canonical `count` op is governed as a distinct executable claim on the active next-target line.
The canonical `exists` op is governed as a distinct executable claim on the active next-target line.
The canonical `bulk_create` op is governed as a distinct executable claim on the active next-target line.

The canonical `bulk_replace` op is governed as a distinct executable claim on the active next-target line.
The canonical `bulk_merge` op is governed as a distinct executable claim on the active next-target line.

Non-conforming transport bypasses are scheduled for removal in the governed transport-dispatch track.
claims.description: App-local HTTP dispatch and similar transport-owned bypasses are explicitly tracked as next-target work to be removed from the transport-dispatch path.

claims.title: The transport-dispatch track is governed as a distinct next-target workstream before implementation delivery begins.
claims.description: The active next-target line includes a dedicated transport-dispatch track with authored ADRs, specs, features, tests, claims, and setup evidence recorded before code delivery starts.

claims.title: REST and JSON-RPC parity remains the governed semantic contract for the shared transport-dispatch path.
claims.description: The transport-dispatch track preserves REST and JSON-RPC parity as a semantic contract and rejects test-only relaxation as the repair strategy.
